summary: A nasal antrostomy is commonly conducted via the inferior meatus using endoscopic techniques targeting the maxillary sinus area. This approach allows direct access while minimizing invasiveness compared to other methods listed. Agent2 reasoned based on standard medical practices involving endoscopy of the sinonasal region where such surgeries occur most frequently.
